title: Reference leak and swallowed exception in C `FunctionWrapper.__init__` when wrapping another FunctionWrapperBase
author: GrahamDumpleton
body:
## Summary

In the C implementation of `FunctionWrapper.__init__`, when the wrapped
object is itself a `FunctionWrapperBase`, the binding is obtained via:

​```c
binding = PyObject_GetAttrString(wrapped, "_self_binding");
​```

This has two defects:

1. **Reference leak.** `PyObject_GetAttrString` returns a new strong
   reference, and `WraptFunctionWrapperBase_raw_init` then `Py_INCREF`s
   `binding` again before storing it. The original new reference is never
   released, so one reference to the binding string object (e.g.
   `"function"`, `"instancemethod"`) is leaked every time a
   `FunctionWrapper` wraps another `FunctionWrapperBase`.

2. **Swallowed exception / missing NULL check.** The return value of
   `PyObject_GetAttrString` is not checked. If the lookup fails with a
   non-`AttributeError` (e.g. `MemoryError`), the code falls through into
   the type-sniffing fallback with a live exception still set, leading to
   the exception being lost or misattributed to an unrelated branch.

The fix should release the new reference after `raw_init` and only fall
through to the fallback when the exception is `AttributeError`, matching
the pattern already used for the sibling `__self__` lookup.

## Credits

Thanks to @devdanzin for the report via:

* https://gist.github.com/devdanzin/e1ac0c43dc4d4278ee82455da4859872
